channel.title: tree on Dorin Fodor
channel.description: Recent content in tree on Dorin Fodor

item.title: Data Structures in Go: Tree
item.description: Introduction A Tree is a non-linear data structure which consists of one or more nodes where a node can have zero or more references to other nodes which will form a sub-tree. There are multiple types of trees: ordered, un-ordered, balanced, non-balanced, with two children, with more than two children, etc. For the purpose of this article I have chosen to implement a non-balanced Binary Search Tree, which is an ordered tree with two child nodes.
